rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target component or implementation scope after all subcomponents and connections
Preserve the existing AADL architecture by only adding annex blocks to valid scopes
generic_rules: Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Place assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports in valid component type scopes
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
For implementation contracts, use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable
For output dependency contracts, write a guarantee that relates visible outputs to visible inputs
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
